text: Imidazolium L-camphorsulfonate (L-ImCS) is a metal-free organic molecular ionic crystal: an imidazolium cation paired with a homochiral L-camphorsulfonate anion, linked by N–H···O hydrogen-bonded chains. It crystallizes in the chiral-polar monoclinic space group P2₁ (point group 2) at 173 K and is a molecular ferroelectric, undergoing a 222F2-type ferroelectric phase transition at Tc = 367 K. Ferroelectricity is confirmed directly on L-ImCS by PFM domain imaging, polarization switching, and hysteresis loops; the quasi-static (Berlincourt) piezoelectric coefficient along the polar [010] axis is ±19 pC/N (local PFM: 15.8 pC/N), comparable to triglycine sulfate. L-ImCS is the enantiomer of D-ImCS (PMC-012).
experimental_method: Quasi-static (Berlincourt) method, piezometer (Piezotest PM200), 110 Hz; ferroelectricity confirmed by PFM domain imaging, polarization switching, and hysteresis loops (measured on L-ImCS thin film)
property_ref_title: Organic Enantiomeric Ferroelectrics with High Piezoelectric Performance: Imidazolium L- and D-Camphorsulfonate
